#  📌 Step 9: Define `play_game` function  

This fucntions runs the full game of 1 or mroe rounds and shows summary

1.  This functions takes one argument `filename`
2.  Opens the file 
3.  Reads the file
4.  Closes the file
5.  Initialises:
    - rounds
    - number of games
    - number of wins
    - a dictionary to track wins in across 1 to 6 guesses
6. repeatedly call the appropriate function to play rounds
7.  after each round:
    - check if player won
    - print success or failure message showing the correct word
    - update number of wins if player won
8.  ask the player if they want to continue
    - 'Y' to continue
    - 'N' to stop
    - else print error message and ask again until valid
9.  If player stops playing:
    - print summary showing win percentage and win distribution
10. fucntion ends after printing the summary


<div style="background:#f0f8ff; padding:15px; border-left:5px solid #2c7be5; margin:20px 0;">
<details>
<summary>💡 Hint</summary>

- First open the file and read the words into a list.
- You can use `.read().split()` to turn the file contents into a list of words.
- Do not forget to close the file after reading.

<div style="margin-left:20px;">
<details>
<summary>ℹ️</summary>

- You need counters to keep track of:
  - the round number
  - the total number of games played
  - the total number of wins
- You also need a dictionary to count how many wins happened in 1 guess, 2 guesses, and so on up to 6 guesses.

</details>
</div>

- Use a `while True:` loop so the game keeps running until the player chooses to stop.
- At the start of each round, increase the round number by 1.
- Call `play_more(read, rounds)` to play one round.

<div style="margin-left:20px;">
<details>
<summary>ℹ️</summary>

- The value returned by `play_more(read, rounds)` includes:
  - the game result
  - the chosen word
- You can use indexing to access them.

<div style="margin-left:20px;">
<details>
<summary>⭐</summary>

    game = play_more(read, rounds)
    word = game[1]

</details>
</div>

</details>
</div>

- if the round was won update:
  - number of wins
  - the correct value inside dictionary of wins
- always increase number of games after each round

<div style="margin-left:20px;">
<details>
<summary>ℹ️</summary>

- Ask the user if they want to continue by using input().
- Keep asking until they enter either 'Y' or 'N'.

</details>
</div>

- If the user enters 'N', print the summary and stop the loop.
- The win percentage can be calculated using:

<div style="margin-left:20px;">
<details>
<summary>⭐</summary>

    percentage = round(9number of wins / number of games) * 100)

</details>
</div>

- For the win distribution:
  - if the count is 0, print the number
  - otherwise print `#` repeated that many times, followed by count

</details>
</div>


<div style="background:#fffaf0; padding:15px; border-left:5px solid #FB8C00; margin:20px 0;">
<details>
<summary>📝 Test Code</summary>
```python 
import urllib.request
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/09-test.py"
with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response:
    code = response.read().decode("utf-8")
exec(code)
```
</details>
</div>
